Description: Attackers encrypt central file share and backup shares via compromised admin account
Asset / Process: File server FS-01 + central SMB share
Vulnerability: Flat admin network, shared backup credentials
Existing Controls: EDR, MFA on admin accounts, offline backups, partial network segmentation

Description: Employee enters credentials on cloned M365 login page
Vulnerability: User awareness gap, no phishing-resistant MFA
Existing Controls: Mail filter, awareness training, phishing simulations, MFA (TOTP)

Risk Title: Insider data exfiltration via personal email
Description: Leaving employee sends customer list to private Gmail
Vulnerability: Weak DLP on outbound email, broad CRM access

Description: Critical SaaS provider suffers 8h outage during peak period
Existing Controls: SLA, incident monitoring, manual fallback checklist

Risk Title: GDPR breach via misconfigured S3 bucket
Vulnerability: No IaC review, no scheduled bucket audit
Existing Controls: Quarterly config audit, bucket policy template

Risk Title: Loss of historical marketing assets due to local hardware failure
Description: Old campaign creatives stored on a single workstation are lost when its disk fails
Vulnerability: No automated backup of local creative folder
Existing Controls: Files older than 12 months are not business-critical and can be re-created or are available from agency partners

Risk Title: Storage of cardholder data in own ERP
Description: Storing full PAN in the ERP would bring the company into PCI DSS scope and create high regulatory and reputational exposure
Status: Avoided — payments outsourced to PCI-DSS Level 1 provider (Stripe), no PAN ever touches own systems
